An immersive exhibition, a sensory experience, unique and innovative, which combines the work of art with moving image and sound.
The first immersive exhibition in Portugal dedicated to the work of a Portuguese artist.
The musician and composer Rodrigo Leão signs the original soundtrack, specially created for this animation of the painting by Maria Helena Vieira da Silva.

Japan: Festivities and Traditions
The celebration of the New Year, the Boys and Girls Parties, the purification, protection and funerary rites, as well as the fertility cults and the end of year ceremonies are some of the festivities and rituals of Japan, a country full of religious traditions mostly originating in Shintoism, to which practices from Buddhism and Christianity were added.
The exhibition Japan: Festivities and Traditions proposes an immersive journey to these festivities.
Among set designs, recreations of altars and cult spaces, objects of devotion, statuettes and pictures, there are more than 1500 pieces to see.

Organised around large installations, the exhibition is defined by the rhythm they set and the connection found with other neighbouring artworks.
This display corresponds, in a fluid way, to a group of ideas that framed the choice of works: transgression (of the Portuguese dictatorship), manifesto (the first Portuguese artistic ecological manifesto), interiority (of the experience drawn from the artwork), mutation (technological, post-human) and evocation (of a real connection with the living world).
The majority of the contemporary works are in dialogue with a selection of modernist works, illustrating the time range of CAM’s collection and to create lines of tension between the different periods it covers.
‘Tide Line’ includes a variety of paintings, drawings, video, photography and sculptures.

The exhibition ‘Deconstructing Colonialism, Decolonising the Imaginary. Portuguese Colonialism in Africa: Myths and Realities’ will be on display to the public in the largest temporary exhibition room of the National Museum of Ethnology between 30 October 2024 and 2 November 2025.
The exhibition, co-organised by the National Museum of Ethnology (Museus e Monumentos de Portugal, E.P.E.) and the Centre for African and Development Studies CEsA (Instituto Superior de Economia e Gestão, UL), takes place in the context of the Museum’s priority to study the provenance of its extra-European collections and to reflect on the colonial context in which the museum was founded and its first collections were gathered, seeking to involve the public and communities in the appreciation and dissemination of their own cultures.
Conceived and coordinated by historian Isabel Castro Henriques, the exhibition aims to present the outlines of Portuguese colonialism in Africa in the 19th and 20th centuries. It aims to deconstruct the myths created by colonial ideology, decolonise Portuguese imagery and contribute, in an educational and accessible way, to a renewal of knowledge about the Portuguese colonial question.
Two central axes structure the exhibition’s narrative. The first axis is organised in thematic panels, in which text and image are articulated, highlighting the outlines of Portuguese colonialism in the 19th and 20th centuries, and giving the word to historical knowledge. The second axis aims to ‘make African works of art speak’, as material evidence of African thought and culture, highlighting the organisational complexity of the social and cultural systems of these societies, making it possible to show African creativity, vitality, wisdom, rationality, identity diversity and skills, and helping to highlight and deconstruct the falsifying nature of Portuguese colonial myths.
This second axis of the exhibition is formed by a selection of 139 works, divided between the collections of the National Museum of Ethnology, including some pieces on deposit from the Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation and the collector Francisco Capelo, and works of contemporary African art by the artists Lívio de Morais, Hilaire Balu Kuyangiko and Mónica de Miranda.
Conducted as part of the 50th anniversary celebrations of the 25 April Revolution, this project is the result of the work carried out by a team of around thirty researchers who collaborated on it, as well as the indispensable contribution of many national and foreign entities, who provided the profuse iconographic documentation presented in the explanatory panels around which the exhibition’s narrative is developed.
The exhibition’s Executive Committee is chaired by Isabel Castro Henriques and includes Inocência Mata, Joana Pereira Leite, João Moreira da Silva, Luca Fazzini and Mariana Castro Henriques, and its Scientific Committee, also chaired by Isabel Castro Henriques, is made up of 20 members, including António Pinto Ribeiro, Aurora Almada Santos, Elsa Peralta, Isabel do Carmo and José Neves.
The museography, installation and presentation to the public of all the works in the National Museum of Ethnology’s collections was carried out by the Museum’s own team, which also ensured the production of the exhibition, with the collaboration of the team from Museus e Monumentos de Portugal, E.P.E. The Communication and Exhibition Project was developed by P 06 studio.
The parallel programme to be developed between 2024 and 2025 as part of this project includes a travelling exhibition, exclusively for documentational purposes, which will be shown in schools and cultural centres in Portugal, as well as in various Portuguese-speaking areas in Africa and Brazil. Also in 2024, as part of this parallel programme, a film cycle entitled “Cinema and Decolonisation” will take place, with screenings of films related to the post-colonial reality, at ISEG and the National Museum of Ethnology, and other scientific activities are planned, namely Conferences and Colloquia, also in partnership with other entities.
The exhibition is accompanied by the publication of a book of the same name, published by Edições Colibri, expanding the various themes covered in the exhibition by the researchers who collaborated on the project.

Inaugurated fifteen years ago in Cascais, the Casa das Histórias Paula Rego, a monographic museum dedicated to Portugal's best-known contemporary artist, is a landmark in the country's cultural panorama.
At the same time, the Casa das Histórias Paula Rego Collection began to be built, the focus of the new exhibition ‘The CHPR Collection in dialogue’.
The exhibition is an initiative of the D. Luís I Foundation and Cascais Town Council as part of the museum's anniversary celebrations. It can be seen from 31 October.

The 331 Amoreiras in Metamorphosis exhibition marks the 30th anniversary of the Arpad Szenes-Vieira da Silva Foundation, where the Marquês de Pombal ordered the planting of 331 mulberry trees to feed the silk factory’s transformation and production process.
Inspired by the textile thinking that characterises Vieira da Silva’s work, and takes as its starting point the idea of metamorphosis, solidarity and interdependence between the plant (the mulberry tree), the animal (the silkworm and its pupa) and the human (the weavers), in order to weave dialogues with the works on display and the physical space.

The first immersive biographical story dedicated to a national personality.
A journey of emotions hand in hand with one of the most inspiring women of all time. A dynamic and everlasting experience that will remain in the memory, beyond the boundaries of time and space.
An experience that only makes sense to us: sensorial, alive. One that crosses all the senses and emotions - the sound, the visual, the virtual, the immersive and the unexpected.
Over 700m2, in the city's new cultural centre in Marvila, we invite visitors, guided by the fado singer's voice, to embark on a unique journey through the artist's material and immaterial estate, scenographic installations, cutting-edge technology, dynamic and interactive content, combined with a storytelling that combines the physical with the digital.
In the eight rooms of this exhibition, we combine different thematic environments, where you can get to know Amália in her various dimensions - artistic, social, personal and international.
What you'll find
Virtual Reality
Crossing a high-impact scenic installation with a virtual reality experience, the physical and digital dimensions come together to provide a unique experience in which you can live the Amália of yesterday, today and forever.
360º Room
In this room we get to know Amália through an immersive 360º experience, using multimedia and videomapping, allowing the viewer to feel part of this "Strange Way of Life".
Hologram
With a full-scale hologram and scenography that replicates the Olympia stage in Paris, the public will have the unique opportunity to witness a performance by the fado singer.
Ah, Amália - Living Experience brings together SP Entertainment and the Amália Rodrigues Foundation for the voice of a culture.  This exhibition would not be possible without the inexhaustible support of the Amália Rodrigues Foundation, whose mission is to promote and publicise Amália's contribution to the affirmation of national identity.

The Terreiro do Paço Handicrafts Market is one of Lisbon’s latest markets. Located in the city’s most iconic square, it is a genuine national exhibition of handicrafts, both traditional and urban.
Exhibitors show their creations and production processes in an event that brings art and people closer together.
The arcades in Terreiro do Paço take on a different life during this meeting between artisans, locals and tourists.
And Martinho da Arcada, the oldest café in Lisbon, always has an inviting seat where you can rest after shopping.
You’ll also find countless inviting and comfortable outdoor cafés and restaurants to sit and admire the beauty all around.
Come and meet the vendors of the products on sale and the amazing marketplace where they are on show every weekend and public holiday.

MUSEU BANKSY aims to bring the artist's message to a wider audience, especially in cities where his work has not yet appeared. Art lovers in Lisbon can enjoy Banksy's street art in one place and contemplate his ideas on various political themes such as war, peace, individualism, greed, poverty, capitalism, consumerism, and hypocrisy.
The museum showcase 100 reproductions of Banksy's artwork through video installations, graffiti, canvases, projections, and murals that shed new light on many universal issues. Visitors explore through different rooms, arranged by country, traveling from the UK to France, passing by the USA, Palestine/Israel, and most recently, Ukraine, our latest addition to the collection.
A unique immersive experience offering a journey through Banksy's world.

The most luxurious and sophisticated avenue in Lisbon is also the stage for one of the most emblematic fairs in the city. The Avenida Fair has been held since 2011.
Every second weekend of the month, the Feira na Avenida occupies a part of the Avenida da Liberdade with its traditional and auteur handicrafts, antiques, design objects, various antique books and collector’s items alongside organic and gourmet food producers that will delight you.
During the months the sun shines most, the fair also takes place on the fourth Saturday of the month and the preceding Thursday.
There are thus many opportunities to shop on the Avenue, even without going into the most exclusive stores.

The Príncipe Real market is one of the most popular for handicrafts and bric-a-brac in Lisbon. It takes place on the last Saturday and Monday of each month and is the place to find unique antiques and handicrafts from various periods and provenance in the lovely and calm surroundings of the park.
On Saturday mornings there is also an organic market in Príncipe Real with producers from all around the country selling their top quality produce. It’s well worth spending the morning amongst the stalls buying freshly picked fruit or articles for the home from the last century. This is another way to discover the way Lisbon lives.

Fado is Portuguese and was born in Lisbon. It was adopted by Portuguese culture and is now a symbol of Portugal, with a strong tradition in the historic districts of Lisbon.
The hour-long Fado in Chiado show features talented artists – a singer and two musicians (Portuguese guitar and classical guitar) – performing Fado and the music that makes it the national song.
The live performances are accompanied by images of unique must-visit locations in Lisbon. It is said that Fado combines nostalgia (saudade), Portuguese pride, hope and conformism in a musical style epitomised by Amália Rodrigues. On account of these unique characteristics, Fado has now been declared part of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO.
Fado in Chiado takes place from Monday to Saturday in the very centre of Lisbon. The hour-long show is the ideal suggestion for seeing live Fado and then taking advantage of the night to discover more of the city.
Discover Fado in Chiado and you will find the soul of Lisbon
From Monday to Saturday • 17.30 and 19:00 • 50 minutes duration

If you come to Lisbon on holiday, there’s no reason to neglect a healthy diet.
Whether you’ve rented a house and are looking for the best food possible or just want to discover the taste of the local fruit, the Mercado Biológico Agrobio de Lisboa, in Campo Pequeno, sells various types of produce by different certified Portuguese farmers.
You’ll find vegetables, aromatic plants, fruit, legumes and even essential oils and cosmetics essential for a more sustainable and healthy life.
Every Saturday, in the garden of Campo Pequeno, you’ll find stalls full of the authentic, certified and organic flavours of Lisbon – and Portugal.

The Republican National Guard (GNR - Guarda Nacional Republicana), promotes, on the
third Sunday of each month
, at 11:00 am, the Solemn Changing the Guard at the National Palace of Belém, which is often described as one of the most beautiful military ceremonies of its kind in the world. It is a ceremony clad with great symbolism and tradition, being carried out by the military that guarantee the permanent security to the Official Residence of the President of the Republic.
The Solemn Render of the Guard arises, returning to an old military tradition, outside the Palace, where it still takes place today. Its essence is to make known to the population the symbolism of the passing of witness between two Guards who take turns in guarding the Palace. In the ceremony, there are 160 cavalry and 4 infantry soldiers, constituting the Guards to the GNR Palace, the Music Band and the Fanfare, the Charameleiros, a Horse Squad that escorts the bearer of the Santo-e-Senha and Charanga da GNR.
The beginning of the military ceremony is announced by a trumpet suit, which performs the "royal innings". The ceremony unfolds with the Guards, at the same time, to take position in front of the palace: first that will be surrendered and soon that surrenders. After the two Guards have been formed and after the "President's trumpet call", the National Anthem is played. After this solemn act, the commanders of the Guards move to the center, greeting each other and transmitting orders or information important to the service they have finished/will perform. Then formally begins the Changing the Guard.
After approximately 1 hour of ceremonial, the public is invited to attend a performance of Charanga on horseback in Jardim Vieira Portuense, where Charanga offers the presents playing musical excerpts, simultaneously with the execution of a carousel with several evolutions, namely, the step, trotting, but also galloping, being the only Charanga on horseback in the world that acts in this progress.

In one of Lisbon’s oldest residential neighbourhoods, you’ll find the Benfica handicrafts market.
Because it’s not just home to a world famous football club; it’s also a neighbourhood full of life and commerce.
On the second Saturday of every month, it gets more interesting, with the holding of this special market. Near Nilo patisserie on the Estrada de Benfica you’ll find artisans, artists and small producers selling all kinds of things connected to their areas.
Patchwork, crochet, rag bags and ceramics are just some of the examples of what you can find in this calm and tree-lined area of Benfica beside the church.
Discover how people live outside the centre of the city and take a unique souvenir of Lisbon home with you.

For 27 years, he devised four proposals for the construction of a Palace of the City to crown the top of Parque Eduardo VII. On the 50th anniversary of his death, this exhibition shows lesser-known aspects of these projects which, although they never came to fruition, reveal his evolution as an architect and the urbanistic thinking he developed about Alto do Parque Eduardo VII.
A municipal architect for a decade (1938-1947) and on his own after that, the author developed several projects that changed the face of the city and gave it modernity and monumentality that are still present today, such as the first version of the airport.
The urban and landscape interventions in Monsanto Forest Park and Campo Grande are two manifestations of a civic action that aimed to shape mentalities, transform social behaviour and force local decision-makers to plan the city.
This exhibition, featuring objects from the Lisbon Museum, the Lisbon Municipal Archive and the Gabinete de Estudos Olisiponenses [Olisiponense Studies Office], also highlights the constant relationship that existed between different architects and the never-realised projects for the extension of Avenida da Liberdade and the Palace of Justice.
In addition to the unrealised plan to extend the Pimenta Palace – a long-standing ambition of several generations of leaders of the Museum of the City and the current Museum of Lisbon – this exhibition will also provide an insight into Keil do Amaral's profound intervention for a Lisbon that is, after all, constantly changing.

This cultural space provides access to the murals by Almada Negreiros, the largest collection from the 20th century.
Here, you can find 14 mural panels, six at the Rocha do Conde de Óbidos Maritime Terminal and eight at the Alcântara Maritime Terminal.
At the latter, visitors can explore nine rooms featuring digital and interactive experiences about the construction and decoration context of the Navigation Terminals.
You can also explore the artistic legacy left by Almada Negreiros in Lisboa, as well as documentation about the Maritime Terminals, including testimonies, interviews, notes, photographs, and reproductions of works and documents.

In 1755, a violent earthquake devastated Lisbon and created a before and after in the archaeological record, with testimonies and experiences of the city of the 16th, 17th and 18th centuries trapped under the rubble. The highly coherent faience collection at the Museum of Lisbon – Roman Theatre is a clear expression of the cataclysm.
The characteristic white and blue has been transformed into a black glaze that mimics the colour of the fire, giving these faiences a very different appearance from pieces from other places. These differences are visible in the comparison between the sets presented in this temporary exhibition. Organised into three sections – Casa de Fresco, the middle floor of the museum, and the archaeological area on the lower floor – each of these areas addresses a different aspect of the theme: the period's predilection for faience and the ostentatious display of wealth; the origins and characteristics of faience; the fire marks on the pieces and their archaeological contexts.

June 7 to October 12
With curiosity and rigor, Vivienne Westwood (1941-2022) continuously delved into the past to assert her identity and create some of the most original looks in contemporary fashion.
Westwood is famous for her subversive approach to emblematic elements of British culture.
Skilled in technique and garment construction, the English designer reinvented elements like crinoline, bustle (or tournure), and corsetry, proposing sculptural and assertive silhouettes.

Museum of Lisbon - Pimenta Palace
25 JUN'25  >>>  22 MAR'26
Pieces of exceptional quality, for various reasons these works have been kept away from the public eye, some never before shown and others have been restored to be exhibited and thus reveal a less obvious Lisbon.
The route starts in Terreiro do Paço, where we remember the aeronautical feat performed by an Italian captain in 1794, and ends nearby, in Campo das Cebolas, in the company of Amália Rodrigues.
Among other places and episodes, it passes through the capital's first illegal neighbourhood and the first Feira Popular funfair, revealing both a bomber who stirred up the already troubled early years of the Republic, and a poet and human rights activist who in the 19th century fought for the end of slavery and the emancipation of women.
Unique stories on a journey through other universes of Lisbon.

The exhibition pencil for painting grey days – Works from the EDP Foundation Art Collection brings together works by 21 Portuguese artists. The title is based on a work by Carlos Nogueira, dated 1973, which includes a small card with the following typed phrase:
WHEN YOU GAVE ME A COLOURED PENCIL – RED –
WHICH WROTE IN PINK
A PENCIL FOR PAINTING GREY DAYS
It comprises six rooms, four of which are dedicated to as many artists, featuring exclusively works by Carlos Nogueira, Maria José Oliveira, Tomás Colaço and Luísa Correia Pereira. In the remaining two, there are works by Ana Pérez-Quiroga, Ângelo de Sousa, Eduardo Batarda, Eduardo Gageiro, Helena Almeida, Jorge Martins, Jorge Pinheiro, José Loureiro, Luisa Cunha, Manuela Marques, Maria Beatriz, Maria Pia Oliveira, Pedro Gomes, René Bertholo, Rita Magalhães, Rodrigo Oliveira and Tiago Batista. These works share a common idea of generosity, ‘capable of transforming moments and sometimes even days,’ says curator Margarida Almeida Chantre.
The EDP Foundation Art Collection was launched in 2000 with the aim of covering several generations of contemporary Portuguese artists, as well as various areas and disciplines of artistic creation. It currently comprises around 2,540 works by more than 345 artists. Its chronological starting point was the 1960s, a period marked by artistic ruptures which also symbolically corresponds to the date of the creation of the Companhia Portuguesa de Eletricidade, the predecessor of the EDP group.

Featuring lounge chairs, blankets for cooler nights, popcorn, drinks, and a top-tier film selection, it’s the perfect summer night plan.
Curated by film journalist Rui Pedro Tendinha and technically supported by Cinebox, the program blends timeless classics—Grease, E.T., Saturday Night Fever, Pretty Woman—with recent favorites like Ainda Estou Aqui, Anora, A Complete Unknown, and Lilo & Stitch.
Some screenings include special guest appearances, and all offer high-quality immersive sound via headphones.
This rooftop cinema invites all to celebrate film under the stars in a unique and festive setting in the heart of Lisbon.

Miriam Cahn (Basel, 1949) is a prominent figure on the European contemporary art scene. Her work has received widespread critical and institutional acclaim in recent years, following important exhibitions at Palais de Tokyo (Paris), Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía (Madrid), Stedelijk Museum Amsterdam, Haus der Kunst (Munich), Documenta 14 (2017) and Venice Art Biennale (1984 and 2022).
Miriam Cahn's artistic practice, in which painting and drawing feature prominently, but which also includes other media such as sculpture, installation, video, and photography, explores topics related to her objection to all forms of oppression and reaction to current social and political issues, including gender violence, migratory problems and armed conflicts. Her work is strongly imbued with the motivations, attitudes and discourses that marked the feminist movements of the 1970s and 80s.
The body is central to her work. According to the curators of the exhibition, João Pinharanda and Sérgio Mah: “The body as a domain with a number of thematic resonances, but also as a creative medium (kindling and adding to certain ways of looking, drawing, painting...), is the core of the artist's imagery.”
This is the artist's first solo exhibition in Portugal, featuring 160 works that primarily showcase her recent works, alongside some selected pieces from the 1970s, 80s and 90s.

João Machado (1942) is responsible for having created numerous projects in editorial design, corporate identity, and philately.
However, the poster assumes autonomy in the consolidation of his authorial work and holds recognised significance within the historiography of this genre in Portugal.
His body of work highlights the importance of drawing and its application to editorial illustration, which — alongside composition and use of colour — is equally distinctive and crucial to the effectiveness of his visual language.
The methodical way João Machado archived records, graphic materials, preliminary versions, mock-ups and final artworks — both in physical and digital formats, in which he was a pioneer — reveals his awareness of the unique value of each element in the construction of his working vocabulary and the evolutionary continuity of models and compositions.
Worthy of mention are his long-standing and fruitful collaborations with CTT — Correios de Portugal and the Cinanima International Animated Film Festival.

The technical and iconographic uniqueness of their work led sculptor João Rolaça to Tamil Nadu, where these monumental sculptures inspired his doctoral thesis on large-scale ceramic sculpture.
The research sparked an exchange of artistic knowledge, including a residency at the Convent Workshops in Montemor-o-Novo.
There, three master Velar artists created the sculptures on show, alongside terracotta sculptures from the Museu do Oriente collection and works by sculptor Liliana Velho.

In 2025, the Lisbon Düsseldorf Initiative, centred on culture and the relationship between two cities - through knowledge, work between citizens, companies and institutions – presents this photography exhibition at the MNAC [PeP] Gallery and later at the Düsseldorf Stadtmuseum.
In black and white, and through the symbolic power of portraiture, two photographers from different backgrounds, Ralf Schilberg, a German portrait photographer, and Rui Soares Esteves, a Portuguese fashion photographer, have aesthetically and freely captured the substance of their own cities, Düsseldorf and Lisbon respectively.
In a dialogue of a plural nature, they created two complementary exhibitions spots - Lisbon Faces and Düsseldorf Faces: identities and alterities; professions and communities.
As observers who produce images, they do separately an approach and recognition of the two cities, arousing curiosity, questions and thoughts about those portrayed and also about the two places. Places that, to quote Cláudio Magris, ‘mean above all people, more or less familiar or almost unknown, but nevertheless witnesses, albeit partial to our existence’.

For centuries, Sintra, known as ‘the place where winter spends the summer’, was the choice of the Royal Family and the nobility to escape the scorching heat of the capital.
To spend the hottest season of the year, they built palaces and planted gardens, where, today, Parques de Sintra offers a diverse programme so that families today, just like those of the past, can enjoy summer to the fullest.
‘An Adventure at the Pena Palace’, staged visits to the Chalet of the Countess of Edla and treasure hunts in the Pena and Monserrate Parks are some of the experiences to enjoy this summer.

The most difficult borders to cross are not marked on any map. Often the real barriers are those of invisibility, drawn by centuries of global colonialism, silence and social exclusion. They are in the eyes, in the bureaucracy, in the archives. They are borders that impose themselves between what is seen and what is recognised, between what is remembered and what is erased. This exhibition seeks to be yet another warning between the subtlety and brutality of racism.
Invisible Borders brings together the unique and provocative perspectives of Alina Zaharia, Gloria Oyarzabal, Grace Ribeiro, Patrik Rastenberger, Omar Victor Diop and Wendel A. White — artists who, through photography, dismantle the architectures of structural racism and reveal its presence in the social, cultural and historical fabric. These works not only denounce explicit and veiled forms of racial exclusion, but also propose modes of resistance, reparation, and reimagination.

Inspired by the collection of the Royal Treasury Museum, artists trained at the Lisbon Jewellery Centre were challenged to reinterpret this heritage, creating works that intertwine tradition and contemporaneity.
The jewels carry echoes of the past. Each piece, a reflection of time, holds memories of kings and queens, celebrations and secrets.
In the heart of the Ajuda National Palace, where diamonds and crowns, necklaces and bracelets tell silent stories, this exhibition gives voice to the jewels. More than precious objects, each creation presented here is an invitation to reflection.
By revisiting the memory of the original pieces, the artists have built bridges between the splendour of the past and the infinite possibilities of the present. Exploring these jewels was an act of research, introspection and discovery. Each line, each material chosen, carries a meaning, an intention.
Thus, ‘Ecos Reais’ is not just a competition, a jewellery exhibition, but an open dialogue, a space where the visitor's gaze completes the narrative.
As you walk through this room, don't just see shapes and sparkles. Listen. Allow yourself to hear the stories these pieces want to tell.

The exhibition “just one world” invites the public to immerse themselves in the uniqueness of the photographic work of Derek Charlwood, a scientist and artist who, throughout his life, combined two seemingly distinct passions: medical entomology and photography.
Charlwood devoted decades of his career to studying the ecology of malaria-transmitting mosquitoes, travelling to remote villages and regions in Africa, South America, Oceania and Southeast Asia. But beyond science, his gaze also captured the human essence of the places he visited. Armed with a camera, he transformed his fieldwork into a sensitive record of the daily lives of the people he encountered, revealing the simple and unexpected beauty that emerges when we take the time to observe.
Inspired by poets and philosophers such as Fernando Pessoa, Charlwood reflects on the nature of beauty and its spontaneous discovery. ‘If we don’t look for it, we find it,’ he writes, reminding us that beauty is in the eye of the beholder. This perspective is also close to Zen wisdom: ‘Life without purpose loses its meaning.’ His scientific purpose – to understand disease-carrying insects – gave him access to a broader dimension: the understanding that, regardless of geography or social status, we all share the same humanity.
“just one world” is, therefore, more than just an exhibition of documentary photography. It is a humanistic testimony that echoes the tradition of great post-war masters of photography, such as Sebastião Salgado. In the faces, expressions and gestures captured by Charlwood, we find not only the rigour of a documenting scientist, but above all the empathy of someone who recognises, in each individual, the universal dignity of the human condition.
The title of the exhibition echoes a fundamental conviction: we are, in fact, just one world. Science taught him the interdependence of species and ecosystems; art showed him the universality of emotions and human relationships. Together, these two dimensions construct a powerful narrative about unity, respect and sharing.

How Heavy is a City?
This question instigates a three-year research project into the complex set of transformations of both the city and its backdrop, revealing a new figure in the making with a magnitude of planetary dimensions. To make sense of these transformations and sort out the underlying agencies, a re-assembly of architecture and what it means to build a collective environment is required.
The next edition of the Triennale aims to open a public space of learning, experience, curiosity, inquiry, debate, delight, outrage, speculation, transgression, imagination and action about the possible futures of cohabitation. How Heavy is a City? explores emerging forms of cooperation and mutuality, establishing a new unit to evaluate architecture to recast its role as a driver of debate.
Ann-Sofi Rönnskog and John Palmesino, founders of Territorial Agency, are the Chief Curators, leading an investigation of the spatial dimensions of the transformations marking the Anthropocene by asking questions of design, governance, agency and proposition. Opening in the Autumn, the Triennale 2025 acts as a convener, engaging a broad team of experts from science to philosophy or the arts ​​who participate from the outset in a programme branched off into three lines of research – Fluxes, Spectres, and Lighter – each one resulting in an exhibition, book chapter and talk series.

The MURMUR project is part of a programme of temporary exhibitions dedicated to presenting works by Portuguese and foreign artists, designed especially for the walls of the MACAM Grand Hall. In this second edition, curated by Carolina Quintela, Portuguese artist Isabel Cordovil (Lisbon, 1994) presents an installation in which she seeks to explore the tension between inherited identity and desired identity, between language and love, norm and otherness.
Cordovil's work investigates the politics of revisiting narratives — whether in myth, folklore, religion, literature, dreams or around the collective unconscious — and the new navigations or manipulations possible of them. Mainly through installation and sculpture, her work reflects a poetic language of playful metaphor, visual freedom and disobedience, while seeking new ways of belonging/challenging and celebrating otherness.

On the south shore of the Tejo, Barreiro is preparing to host OUT.FEST, considered to be one of the most important experimental music events in the country.
OUT.FEST - Barreiro International Exploratory Music Festival is an annual festival whose programming seeks to reflect the most significant developments in contemporary experimental music in its many forms - from improvised music to abstract electronics, from free-jazz to noise, from contemporary classical music and the new and unclassifiable musical languages born every day throughout the world. Alongside music, the festival features a broad and exciting universe of experiences and multi-disciplinary events bustling with novelty, challenging the classical rules and borders of the many forms of human and artistic expression.
From the need for an event highlighting the immense importance of all this activity on the edges and front-lines of our contemporary culture, OUT.FEST is born. From the outskirts of Lisbon, one of the country's great cultural centres, an almost perfect parallel between the periphery of the artistic "mainstream" is drawn, a fertile region and a true laboratory of the great art of our times.

Come and discover Lisbon’s most original market. The Feira da Ladra, or Flea Market, which is immortalised in the poem by the Portuguese singer songwriter Sérgio Godinho, is held every Tuesday and Saturday in Campo de Santa Clara, behind the monastery of São Vicente de Fora.
Traditionally dedicated to selling used objects, you’ll find real gems amongst indescribable bric-a-brac.
The market dates back to 1272 and was held in various parts of the city before settling permanently here.
From here you can easily visit the Panteão Nacional and the church of São Vicente de Fora, so the best thing to do is save this neighbourhood for a day when the market is on.
Between souvenirs and bargains, you may just take the best memory of your stay in Lisbon from here.

Portuguese Symphony Orchestra
with Cristina Branco, Raquel Tavares and Ricardo Ribeiro
In 1952, Amália Rodrigues won over American audiences when she sang at the La Vie en Rose nightclub in New York. Her success was repeated at prestigious venues such as the Hollywood Bowl, Lincoln Centre and Carnegie Hall.
Amália's repertoire in the US combined traditional fados, fados-canção by Frederico Valério, traditional Portuguese songs and hits from Broadway and Hollywood. In 1965, she recorded two albums: Amália Canta Portugal, with traditional songs orchestrated by Joaquim Luís Gomes, and Amália na Broadway, released in 1984, with songs from the Great American Songbook.
In 1966, she was a soloist in symphonic concerts with the New York Philharmonic Orchestra and the Los Angeles Philharmonic Orchestra, performing traditional Portuguese songs and fados, to great acclaim.
Amália's programme in America pays tribute to the fado singer's important connection to that country, through a repertoire composed of new orchestral arrangements. Premiered in September 2024 at the Belém Cultural Centre, the works are now being presented on the occasion of the closing of the 40th anniversary of the Luso-American Development Foundation at Carnegie Hall, the legendary venue that is sponsoring the debut of the Portuguese Symphony Orchestra in New York.

Mr. Paul is an unusual man: he hasn’t left his home in years, and spends his days sat down, in extreme lethargy, resistant to any change or movement. A young man, who inherited the property Mr. Paul lives in with his sister, is looking to evict them to set up a lucrative business in the building. However, he didn’t count on Mr. Paul’s immovability and intelligence, which lead to a fierce battle with an unexpected outcome.
O Senhor Paul
discusses the immobility of the status quo, as well as the advances in the so-called progress, with the example of the transformations regarding housing in big cities, or gentrification, which has become a global phenomenon. Written in 1993 by German author Tankred Dorst, this play now acquires a new relevancy, with surprising power dynamics and unusual forms of resistance.

Aquela Canção Sobre o Fim
(That Song About the End) tells the disastrous story of a reunion of high school friends who gradually realise that they can no longer maintain a harmonious relationship. Or might it be about something more than just a simple dinner?
A sharp comedy, a contemporary tragedy based on Greek mythology, as well as a reflection on ‘cancel culture’ and a critique of the hypocrisy of contemporary relationships based on ‘likes’ and ‘dislikes.’
It all begins with a story like so many others, we could even call it an ‘old narrative formula’: years after going their separate ways, a group of friends gather for dinner at Ana’s house, the hostess who has always been the link between them.
The reunion brings up nostalgic memories and reconciliations, but also reveals unresolved tensions and hidden secrets that hang in the air like a dense and heavy atmosphere. As the night progresses, superficial conversations begin to intertwine with subtle provocations and misunderstandings, bringing up wounds that have not healed.
What should be a moment of celebration becomes an unstable dance between old ties and new rivalries, and as the tension grows, the friends are confronted with truths about themselves and their relationships.
In
Aquela Canção Sobre o Fim
, the audience is invited to reflect on the bonds that unite us and what can divide us, showing that the past is never completely behind us, and how everything can change at any moment – and in this show, nothing is what it seems and there will be many twists and turns.

Our relationship with old family photographs and photo albums is deeply emotional. When we leaf through old photographs, we are confronted with good and precious memories, but also sometimes with painful issues. Especially when family history contains difficult or silenced episodes, photographs arouse a variety of contradictory emotions. They have a special power to activate our minds. They can linger in our memory as if demanding a response.
The three artists featured in this exhibition have in common the fact that an essential part of their projects consists of old family photographs they have found. The photographs and archives have motivated them and constitute a substantial part of their series of work. The artists are Aline Motta, Sofia Yala and Yassmin Forte, whose family histories are, in different ways, marked by Portuguese colonialism in Brazil, Angola and Mozambique.

Where: Casa Fernando Pessoa, Lisbon
When: October 17, 18, and 19
On the 17th, 18th, and 19th of October, Casa Fernando Pessoa will host another edition of Lisbon Revisited - Poetry Days - a festival that has, since 2018, been on the rise as a meeting point between poets from different geographies.
This year's edition includes the poets Bernardo Pinto de Almeida (Portugal), Filipa Leal (Portugal), Inês Lourenço (Portugal), Katerína Iliopoúlou (Greece), Najwan Darwish (Palestine), Nuno Moura (Portugal) Slobodan Ivanović (Montenegro) and Stella Nyanzi (Uganda).
For some of these poets, this will be their debut in Portugal, with unpublished translations prepared for the festival.
For three days, there will be readings and conversations around poetry. At the center point of the program, on Saturday, a concert by Manuela Azevedo and Hélder Gonçalves (Clã) brings music into the arms of words.
Lisbon Revisited - Poetry Days is an opportunity to meet and listen to poets from different countries and realities, who join a new audience to share stories, talk about writing, and read their poems out loud. A moment of encounter, exchange and proximity through poetry.

Lisbon Marathon
This marathon is already considered as one of the most beautiful races in the world and acclaimed by international media such as the Forbes Magazine, the Huffington Post and American Express.
You may walk or run in this amazing courses.
Marathon - 42K
The 42 kms distance Marathon sets off from Carcavelos and follows the Coastal Road (Estrada Marginal), firstly by the sea and then along the river until the finish line at Lisbon, Praça do Comércio.
Starting in Carcavelos and finishing at Praça do Comércio, this course is 100% sea and river side, providing to the runners an unique view along the race.
Half Marathon - 21K
The Half Marathon sets off from the
Vasco da Gama Bridge
and charts a 21kms course that also finishes up at
Praça do Comércio
. It provides the opportunity to cross on foot one of the longest bridges in Europe.
The 21km course, takes runners from the north area of the city to downtown Lisbon, crossing emblematic historical monuments and river side views.
8K
The Mini Marathon has its departure at the famous Vasco da Gama bridge and the finish line is at the Parque das Nações.
New Generation
Shorter distance races which are within the reach of all those taking part, whether running or walking, since the main objective is taking part and meeting people.

The three-act drama by Giacomo Puccini, with a libretto by Giuseppe Giacosa and Luigi Illica, based on the play of the same name by Victorien Sardou, is now being performed at the Coliseu dos Recreios.
Premiered in Rome in 1900, this opera marks a high point of the so-called Italian "Giovine Scuola" — a movement that transformed opera with more realistic and intensely dramatic themes.
Staged in a politically troubled Rome at the beginning of the 19th century, Tosca tells the tragic passion between the singer Floria Tosca and the painter Mario Cavaradossi, persecuted by the ruthless police chief, Baron Scarpia. It is a story of love, jealousy, power and sacrifice, guided by a captivating score, where immortal arias such as “Vissi d’arte”, “E lucevan le stelle” and “Recondita” shine harmony”.
This show is a unique opportunity to experience, live, a masterpiece that fuses lyricism with cinematic suspense, in a grand staging that honors Puccini's legacy and the intensity of this passionate tragedy.

Opera in three acts, with subtitles in Portuguese and English.
Finally making its national debut, American composer Samuel Barber's masterpiece, Vanessa (1957).
Vanessa has spent the last twenty years waiting for a former lover whom she blindly believes will return. She holds her family hostage to this vain hope, until her former lover supposedly returns. Is he real or an impostor? Will Vanessa want to continue living in the web she has created for herself, or will she finally face reality?
The composer of the famous adagio offers us an opera with the most inspired and vibrant music, in a libretto by Gian-Carlo Menotti, about illusion and shattered dreams, the passage of time and the refusal to face the harsh reality of life and human relationships.
Performed by soprano Catarina Molder, it is directed by German Daniela Kerck and musically directed by Diogo Costa.

OneRepublic have just announced the "Escape to Europe" tour for 2025, set to make a stop at MEO Arena on November 16th. The US band will feature Ella Henderson for the first half of the show, whose UK #1 debut single was co-written by OneRepublic frontman Ryan Tedder.
This summer, OneRepublic released their sixth studio album, Artificial Paradise, which includes huge hits like “RUNAWAY” and “I Ain’t Worried” (featured in the film Top Gun: Maverick and with over 3 billion streams). The album also includes the latest collaboration with David Guetta, “I Don’t Wanna Wait,” which has accumulated nearly a billion streams since its release.
GRAMMY®-nominated OneRepublic are Ryan Tedder (singer/songwriter and vocalist), Zach Filkins (guitarist), Drew Brown (guitarist), Brian Willett (keyboardist), Brent Kutzle (bassist and cellist) and Eddie Fisher (drummer). The band released their debut album Dreaming Out Loud in 2007, which included the hit “Apologize.” In 2009, they released Waking Up with the hits "All the Right Moves", "Secrets" and "Good Life". In 2013, the platinum album Native brought the hit "Counting Stars". To date, they have accumulated over 5 billion streams on Spotify. In 2021, they released Human, with singles such as "Someday" and "Rescue Me". In 2023, they released "Dear Santa" and "Runaway", among others. In collaboration with David Guetta, they released "I Don't Wanna Wait" as well as "Fire" with Meduza and Leony for UEFA Euro 2024.

At the age of 80, Salvatore Adamo continues to prove that music is, and always will be, his life. After celebrating his six-decade career in 2023, the renowned singer returns to Lisbon on 16 November for a unique concert at the Coliseu dos Recreios as part of his ‘C'est ma vie !’ tour. - an intimate journey through the songs that have marked generations.
On stage, he promises to revisit the songs that have made his history. From timeless classics such as ‘Tombe la Neige’, ‘La Nuit’, ‘Mes Mains sur tes Hanches’ or ‘Inch'Allah’, to the most recent songs from his new album: ‘Des nefs et des groseilles’ - due for release in October this year - the Italo-Belgian singer reflects the diversity and sensitivity of a career that has crossed generations with artistic honesty and elegance.
This will undoubtedly be a special evening with one of the most beloved voices in French-speaking music, in a reunion with the Portuguese public full of emotion, memories and gratitude.

There will also be room to try next generation simulators and to participate in some of the most popular mobile games.

The acclaimed orchestra returns to Portugal in 2025.

“Ovo” – whose name curiously remains in Portuguese and in the original – tells the story of a hasty race to an ecosystem full of life and color, where insects work, eat, crawl, play, fight and even fall in love.
All this in a huge and grandiose tumult of energy, where the contrast between noise and silence is one of the main ingredients that promises to surprise even the most skeptical spectator.
Life flows with all intensity in this animated universe, until, one day, a mysterious egg appears among the insects. The surprise and curiosity about the iconic object become the perfect plot for a unique story, which, in addition to portraying the cycle of life in its most natural order, addresses the innocent love between a seductive Ladybug and the clumsy insect that hatches from an unusual egg.
OVO is nature without filters. A fictional portrait full of imagination, light, sound and emotion, where each character takes on a role that promises to transform the way we look at nature.

SESIMBRA
Dance, competitions and Samba... and plenty of high jinks are the explosive ingredients for this huge carnival party.
Sesimbra's lively carnival tradition takes the form of parades by the Samba groups and schools, involving over two thousand dancers, and the traditional clowns' parade, one of the world's biggest, where they show off the creativity and innovation of their costumes and masks  in a mass parade full of colour and creativity.
The nights are also livened up with ambient music, concerts and DJs.
The programme also includes the traditional cegadas, who are traditionally dressed people with guitars singing parodies in verse, with the objective of making the audience laugh.

Reservation required
Portuguese designers present their new collections and trends for the next Autumn/Winter, during the Portuguese Fashion Week.
Always a in a festive and creative atmosphere Portuguese designers and brands promise to amaze their audience by the audacity and innovation of their collections, promoting Portuguese fashion of the international fashion and design scene.
Created in 1991, the ModaLisboa project has set out to be the first professional organisation for the presentation of the collections by Portuguese fashion designers.
Revealing a growing international image, every year the festival hosts tens of thousands of visitors and hundreds of journalists, both Portuguese and international.

MONSTRA - Lisboa Animated Film Festival returns for another edition in Portugal, showing audiences the best animation being made from across the world.
Created in 2000 with the intention of celebrating artistic versatility, bringing people from different areas of the arts together and promoting new artistic visions, based on the multidisciplinary medium that is Animated Film, MONSTRA rapidly became a reference on the national scene.
Yearly, films are being shown in competition for Feature Films, Short Films, Student Films, Supershorts, many having their world, European or national premiere.
As usual, a vast selection for all audiences and many workshops and masterclasses from some of the world’s great masters in the art of animation, music and interactive media.

The Lisbon Half-Marathon is back again.! Challenge your speed and enjoy a fast-paced run with a privileged view of the Portuguese capital.
Speaking in competitive terms, the course is flat and very fast, the start is at the tolls’ square of the bridge and the Finish Line is in front of the Jerónimos Monastery.
Circuit
Starting at 25 de Abril Bridge, the athletes run towards Alcântara, via Avenida 24 de Julho, and head towards Praça do Comércio and once they reach Campo das Cebolas, they reverse their route, this time in the direction of Avenida da Índia, with the Jerónimos Monastery as the backdrop. It continues along the Marginal Avenue, following the Tejo River, almost to Dafundo, where they turn round to the finish line, which is located next to Jerónimos Monastery.
Surely one of the most attractive races in the world!

Hans Zimmer enters The Next Level - and takes Europe with him!
The multiple Oscar® and Grammy® winner returns to Europe's biggest arenas in 2025 and 2026 with Hans Zimmer Live - The Next Level. The show will take place in Portugal on 31 March 2026 at the MEO Arena in Lisbon.
‘The Next Level’ promises a completely new show, with innovative electronic soundscapes and a spectacular light production that promises to surpass Zimmer's previous shows.

Sónar Lisboa aims to be a meeting point of advanced music, creativity and cutting-edge technology, bringing together an open minded national and international audience.
Over 3 days, Sónar Lisboa will occupy emblematic venues of the city, with day/night events, focused on the latest trends in dance and electronic music, visual arts or digital culture, presenting established artists and exciting newcomers, in a very unique way.
Festival Sónar Lisboa
Three days of Music, Creativity and Technology

The Millennium Estoril Open is the biggest tennis tournament in Portugal and the only stop of the ATP Tour in the country.
The Estoril Tennis Club welcomes tennis stars from around the world and visitors from all over who fill the stands of the Millennium Stadium.
Come with your family and visit the Smash Area to participate in various activities at the stands of the event's sponsoring brands.
Indulge in the food court with over ten diverse options, stop by the Kids' Place where you'll find loads of fun for the little ones to play and babysitting services.
At the Fun Center, we challenge you to pick up a racket and engage in the many games and challenges we propose.

The IndieLisboa festival will once again bring dozens of films for us to discover over 11 days, as well as debates, workshops, meetings, parties and concerts.
A celebration of independent cinema that values diversity highly, it brings us fiction, documentaries, animation and experimental moviemaking, whether short or feature films.
Besides their retrospectives, a programme of recent films will be shown divided into sections or themes, and unreleased works which could not be seen any other way.
There is also an exclusive section dedicated to youngsters, Indie Júnior, with films, workshops, ateliers and a party with lots of fun and games for kids of all ages.

The Italian Film Festival has become a major event in Lisboa for fans of culture and Italian cinema.
This is already one of the most anticipated festivals with the largest number of followers in the film festival scene.
An event that is highly regarded for the quality of its cultural ramifications as well as the enthusiasm and passion of its organizers.
Created in 2008, the festival was born and raised in Portugal. Based on the work of a team of Portuguese and Italian professionals, it is deeply rooted in Portuguese culture and society.
Its aim is to display works that meet high quality standards, whether they be established creators or from a new generation of filmmakers, whilst keeping to the tradition of bringing to Portugal the very best of Italian cinema.

The most irreverent works by Portugal’s most prestigious cartoonists are once again on display at Celeiro da Patriarcal in Vila Franca de Xira.
As usual, this event has two distinct aspects: a national one, featuring a retrospective of the last year, and an international one, with the presence of a foreign guest.
The exhibition promises not only to entertain the public, but also to make people reflect on the events that marked the previous year.
Already a reference for cartoon lovers, it is also an opportunity to explore the diversity of styles, approaches and perspectives that the authors bring to the event.
Celeiro da Patriarcal, Rua Luís de Camões, 130
(train) Vila Franca de Xira Azambuja/Sintra Line, Vila Franca de Xira Station
(bus) 2306, 2307, 2309
Free entry
cm-vfxira.pt

LAAF – Lisbon Art & Antiques Fair
, opens its doors once again at Cordoaria Nacional.
LAAF - Lisbon Art and Antiques Fair is an event with a long history. The Fair takes place, since its first year, in the emblematic space of Cordoaria Nacional, often being called “Cordoaria’s Fair”.
All disciplines of Art, from Classical Antiquity to the Contemporary, take part in this Fair. In order to ensure a high standard of quality in the pieces presented, before the opening of the Fair all the pieces are analysed by a committee of experts from the most diverse areas, with a keen eye to excluding those that do not meet the conditions for participation in this great event.
At the same time, we develop a set of parallel events with the purpose of creating synergies with other cultural agents.
Associação Portuguesa dos Antiquários
T. +351 213 474 571
apa@apa.pt
www.apa.pt

One of the highlights of the cultural rentrée is Open House Lisboa, and as always organized by the Lisbon Architecture Triennale.
The doors will be open to the best architecture in town on a weekend of guided tours to the most incredible buildings in Lisbon, free and with no booking required.
Annually, each edition proposes a new and careful selection of buildings that lets us see emblematic works in the city, their stories and authors, unveils from palaces to private homes and from museums to studios.

Several venues will put on shows by international and national companies and artists will demonstrate their view of the world, establishing relationships between marionettes and other domains, including video, theatre, visual arts and dance.
As well as puppet theatre there will be talks, exhibitions and workshops on the theme May various locations throughout Lisboa.

The ARCOlisboa International Contemporary Art Fair returns to the Cordoaria Nacional with a selection of galleries and a commitment to Portuguese art and its connections to the international scene.
There will also be conferences on art and art collecting.
ARCOlisboa ... a unique space in which to view, purchase and learn about art.
Cordoaria Nacional
Avenida da Índia

15

Jardins Abertos began with the simple, focussed aim of bringing Nature and the city closer together. Lisbon's gardens are places that can encourage learning and, through this relationship, just like a forest we have grown and become known for opening the gates of the city's most beautiful gardens, revealing their flowers, trees and gardeners.
The Spring Edition of Jardins Abertos Festival will take place on May with a programme including guided visits, workshops and activities for families with selected themes aligned with the various cycles of agricultural production: harvesting, preservation, transformation, but also wastage, including presenting creative solutions and research gardens around the city!
Come and visit the gardens of Lisbon!

Lisboa Green Trail
An event for the whole family to enjoy the Green side of Lisbon!
The Lisbon Green Trail is promoted by the Associação Walk and Run with the usual routes: 22K Long Trail, 12K Short Trail with Canicross and the simple Walk with 7K, and for the little ones the Kids Race.
All races will start from Keil do Amaral Amphitheater and you will have the opportunity to discover the most winding trails of Monsanto Forest Park and the best views over the city and river Tagus.

Intended for those who love motorcycles, speed and movies, the Motorcycle Film Fest, presents more than a dozen films exclusively dedicated to motorcycles and everything around them.
With the aim of praising and celebrating the motorcycle culture beyond the seventh art and as a way of promoting creativity at the same time as it provides an opportunity to bring together motorcycles, pilots, artists, directors and actors, this festival has a diverse programme, with cinema sessions (feature and short films), conversations with national and international guests, exhibitions, night tours, live transmission of sporting events and artistic interventions.
Cinema São Jorge
Av. da Liberdade, 175
Metro: Avenida

The Biggest Hybrid and Electric Car Exhibition, returns to Lisbon on May, it will be in FIL - Lisbon International Fair.
The ECAR SHOW will feature national and international brands, including major car manufacturers and companies from the electric mobility sector.
Visitors will be able to explore a wide range of electrified vehicles, including hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and fully electric cars.
The event also showcases micromobility options such as electric bikes, scooters, and motorbikes.
As usual, one of the main highlights is the unveiling of more the year's new models, presenting the latest technological innovations in the automotive industry.
Several brands will host national premieres in Lisbon, giving attendees the chance to discover the most anticipated launches of the year first-hand.
In addition to the exhibition area, visitors will also have the chance to take part in test drives, experiencing various electric models directly on-site.
With strong participation from major brands and a diverse program, ECAR SHOW reinforces its role as a key event for those following the evolution of electric mobility and looking for more sustainable transport solutions.
For more information and ticket purchases, visit the official website:
ecarshow.pt

KULTURfest returns to Lisboa with music and performances, but also featuring a variety of other parties and activities.
The festival also features an openair cinema.

FIA Lisboa is the largest craftsmanship and multicultural trade market in the Iberian Peninsula and the second largest in Europe.
Handicrafts, gastronomy, natural resources and cultural activities and tourism are some of the areas you can find at this fair, which supports regional development and local culture.
As well as promoting and supporting local cultures, the FIA also promotes the relationship between tradition and modernity, helping with the display and sale of traditional and innovative products.
This is a great chance to see a large range of products and cultures.
Feira Internacional de Lisboa (FIL)
Rua do Bojador (metro: Oriente)

June/July
Yearly, it brings some of the best movies to the gardens of Quinta das Conchas. a programme with a lot of animation and adventure.
Culture and community under the stars is the key theme of CineConchas: summer nights filled with emotion, laughter and reflection, with national and international films for all ages.
Quinta das Conchas, Lumiar
(Metro) Quinta das Conchas
(bus) 717, 736, 796, 798 (day service) and 207 (night service)
Free entry

LISB-ON #Jardim Sonoro is not to be missed by music lovers intent on enjoying a daytime urban vibe with Lisboa as its stage.
Over 3 full days of partying at Jardim Keil do Amaral, (Monsanto) - the city’s most iconic green park bathed by the unique Lisboa’s stunning light - is set to receive the biggest names in electronic music.
Famous for its magical atmosphere and the international audience it attracts, it promises to keep abreast of national and international production, reveal promises and promote a reunion between artists and audiences, bringing Chill out and Techno, Disco, House, Jazz, Soul, Funk and Electronic Pop, without leaving out various DJs and concerts.

The MOGA festival is a fusion of electronic music, beach parties and several events happening simultaneously.
Inspired by the vibrations of the Atlantic, it takes place both in Essaouira, in Morocco, and in Costa da Caparica.
The name derives from the word Mogador, which is how the Portuguese, several centuries ago, called the Moroccan land.
But Moga is also the name of a tribe, a community of nomads, who share values and lifestyles that can be experienced at the festival.
Between sunset parties at the beach, sea parties, brunches, surf and yoga, there are electronic music concerts!

The avenues of the Parque Eduardo VII will be full of life for yet another Lisboa Book Fair!
As with all years, the Book Fair is a great chance to find out about new literature as well as discover antiques and unique works.
You can also find out what is happening behind the words, as many authors – especially, but not only, Portuguese authors – come to the fair to speak to their fans at colloquiums and autograph sessions.
All of this in one of the city’s most beautiful places, with the river Tejo at your feet.

Caparica is hosting the Suncébeat Festival, an eclectic and diverse music festival that has become a gathering of families and friends.
The rhythms of R&B, Soul, Hip Hop and Soulful Hous will be heard on the sands of Costa de Caparica, at Irmão and Sereia beaches.
In SuncéBeat style, the festival will also include boat parties, which will sail along the Tagus river with music from House Music DJs.

Rock in Rio is the largest music and entertainment festival in the world.
The first edition was held in 1985 in Brazil, and today the event is one of the most important in the history of world music.
Its enclosure is a true music theme park with lots of entertainment and various activities, which are much more than the main stage shows - street performances, art shows, exhibitions, dance shows, performances by digital influencers, cosplay, a Ferris wheel and more.

Sumol Summer Fest returns to the São João beach in Costa de Caparica with some of Portugal’s most famous DJs.
Created to celebrate the beach, summer, friends and music, and kicking off the start of the holidays, this festival is renowned for welcoming the hottest season of the year with music, sea and urban culture, including surfing, skateboarding, art and dance.
Parque de Campismo Inatel – São João beach – Costa de Caparica, Av. Afonso de Albuquerque, São João de Caparica
Ferry: Porto Brandão – Trafaria

Tapada da Ajuda, once again, hosts Brunch Electronik, the electronic music festival that presents national and international artists and starts at lunch time.
To celebrate the joy of summer and friendship around electronic music, always in an outdoor environment, the festivalgoers will find two stages, a dancing zone, an area to rest in the shadow and tables around which to talk and socialize.
Tapada da Ajuda - Lagoa Branca
Buses: 729, 760, 723, 742

Ageas Cooljazz is a reference musical event that combines music, nature, comfort and heritage.
The first edition took place in 2004, having as its starting point the offer of a cultural event that made the difference for the comfort of the space, diversified musical program and sustainable commitment.
The Marechal Carmona Park and the Hipódromo Manuel Possolo  are the venues, located in the Cascais Museum District, thus making the experience unique.
location: Praceta Domingos D’Avilez - Av. da República, 2750-844, Cascais

full_description:
NOS Alive returns to the Passeio Marítimo de Algés with another “best line-up ever”!
Year after year, this festival brings to Portugal some of the biggest names in the international music scene.
Dozens of bands and many other events will take one of the several stages, over tree days.
Jazz em Agosto festival presents a wide-ranging and varied programme with top international performers at the Gulbenkian Foundation.

The Sol da Caparica festival shines once again for all lovers of Lusophone music at Parque Urbano da Costa de Caparica.
Over four days, the festival’s four stages will host dozens of artists between pop, hip hop, rock, electronic music and several other styles, the festival rhythm will certainly be attending this celebration of Portuguese music.
O Sol da Caparica is also about dance, surfing and fun for all ages.
Parque Urbano Costa de Caparica
Av. Afonso de Albuquerque, Caparica

The time has come. A new experience.
MEO KALORAMA
, a sustainability festival, brings a new music and art!
The stage is set up in one of the city's biggest urban parks, Parque da Bela Vista (Bela Vista = beautiful view), where the festival gets the inspiration for its name – 'kalorama', which means 'beautiful view' in Greek.
This summer festival in Europe will present top national and international artists to provide three days of much excitement.
Sustainability, innovation, inclusion and music are brought together in a festival which will try to use greener energies, promote local commerce and reuse and recycle resources.
Parque da Bela Vista
, Av. Almirante Gago Coutinho

ALCOCHETE
AUGUST
Deeply rooted in Alcochete’s identity, the Festas do Barrete Verde e das Salinas honour two central aspects of local life: bullfighting and salt.
The programme is rich and varied, with highlights including bull runs through the streets, tributes to the Campino (herdsmen), the Salineiro (salt worker) and the Forcado (bull handler), as well as Alcochetano Day and the solemn procession in honour of Nossa Senhora da Vida.
Days and nights of celebration, filled with music, entertainment and the best of Portuguese food.
Alcochete

cm-alcochete.pt

Queer Lisboa's film and activities program meets the growing permeability of Queer Cinema to the world, no longer restricted to a look at their individual and community realities.
The program of the festival continues to be faithful to the essence of a minority that is distinguished by its form of desire, which has given Queer Cinema over the decades a huge formal and narrative freedom.
Also to better understand and think Queer Cinema, we organize workshops, talks, masterclasses and exhibitions that offer our viewers tools to better analyze it.
With a set of proposals that we believe are encouraging and innovative, Queer Lisboa will continue to accompany the surprising, irreverent and not infrequently avant-garde queer culture.

The meeting point is the Marvila neighbourhood, Lisbon, from 17 to 20 September'26.
Festival Iminente will showcase some of the most exciting names of contemporary visual art and urban culture and with a program full of music, visual arts, dance, urban sports, community projects, discussions, gastronomy ... and much more!
Find out who will be joining us and get ready for four unforgettable days.
The same festival | a new venue

Lisbon Marathon
This marathon is already considered as one of the most beautiful races in the world and acclaimed by international media such as the Forbes Magazine, the Huffington Post and American Express.
You may walk or run in this amazing courses.
Marathon - 42K
The 42 kms distance Marathon sets off from Carcavelos and follows the Coastal Road (Estrada Marginal), firstly by the sea and then along the river until the finish line at Lisbon, Praça do Comércio.
Starting in Carcavelos and finishing at Praça do Comércio, this course is 100% sea and river side, providing to the runners an unique view along the race.
Half Marathon - 21K
The Half Marathon sets off from the
Vasco da Gama Bridge
and charts a 21kms course that also finishes up at
Praça do Comércio
. It provides the opportunity to cross on foot one of the longest bridges in Europe.
The 21km course, takes runners from the north area of the city to downtown Lisbon, crossing emblematic historical monuments and river side views.
8K
The Mini Marathon has its departure at the famous Vasco da Gama bridge and the finish line is at the Parque das Nações.
New Generation
Shorter distance races which are within the reach of all those taking part, whether running or walking, since the main objective is taking part and meeting people.

MIL is back, and with it comes a set of portuguese and international artists with a programme involving the entire city and a combination of concerts, debates, masterclasses and professional meetings that will cast light on the diversity of emerging and alternative artists and projects.
The festival focuses on the dissemination and internationalisation of modern music and sets itself up as a meeting place for music industry agents from all over the world.
Once again, the great comic strip festival brings together the very best of art to Amadora, with exhibitions of the best domestic and foreign authors, lectures, novelties, signing sessions, etc.

Created in 1991, the ModaLisboa project has set out to be the first professional organisation for the presentation of the collections by Portuguese fashion designers.
Revealing a growing international image, every year the festival hosts tens of thousands of visitors and hundreds of journalists, both Portuguese and international.

From improvised music to Abstract Electronics, from free Jazz to Noise, without forgetting Contemporary Classical and the languages revealed to the world each passing day, not yet with ratified classification, such are the many stands that give OUT.FEST.

Every year in the second half of October, doclisboa previews the best documentaries of the last season and brings together a series of non-competitive programmes, including a retrospective of an internationally acclaimed author.
Doclisboa enlivens Lisbon's cinemas with the presentation of a large number of films, many of them unreleased in Portugal.

Et voilà!
The French Film Festival arrives in Lisboa bringing to the capital some premieres of the best French film productions.
There will also be time set aside for rediscovering classics and the program will also feature the screening of premiere films and animations for all audiences.
In various venues in the city and region.
